+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ PROCTER & GAMBLE SECURES TOP SPOT IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS ON FORTUNE'S 2026 INNOVATION LIST

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) has garnered the number one position in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a title it has maintained for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, emphasizing the company's strength in Product Innovation among a field of 300 leading innovators in the United States.The Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, evaluates companies across three critical dimensions: Product Innovation, Process Innovation, and Innovation Culture. This comprehensive assessment ensures that Procter & Gamble's innovative capabilities are recognized in a rigorous manner, placing the company among America's most elite organizations with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion.Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer at Procter & Gamble, stated, "This recognition is a testament to our incredible organization around the world and their commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate." He emphasized the company's consumer-first approach, focusing on deeply understanding consumer needs and employing advanced science and technology to develop solutions that resonate with customers.Procter & Gamble's sustained recognition in Product Innovation reflects substantial investments across its diverse brand portfolio. The company continues to integrate innovation strategies across various categories, including Fabric Care, Home Care, and Personal Health Care. This approach enables Procter & Gamble to leverage its extensive intellectual property portfolio, translating scientific advancements into products that enhance consumer lives.As an industry leader, Procter & Gamble's commitment to innovation is further demonstrated in its robust performance in the Fortune 500 rankings. The company's diverse range of trusted brands, including Tide®, Gillette®, and Olay®, solidifies its position as a key player in the global market, serving consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information on Procter & Gamble and its initiatives, please visit https://www.pg.com.

+ P&G PRO REVIVES MR. CLEAN: A NEW ERA FOR PROFESSIONAL CLEANING SOLUTIONS

CINCINNATI--Mr. Clean is back from retirement—and with Mr. Clean PRO, he’s ready for hire as businesses’ most dependable new employee with a full cleaning portfolio to get the job done. Today, 10th April 2026, Mr. Clean PRO is introducing the next generation of professional cleaning to help businesses clean more efficiently and effectively, making him the employee businesses can always count on.Cleaning is a critical aspect of operations across various sectors, including restaurants, retail, and offices. Mr. Clean PRO aims to address frequent challenges faced by professionals, particularly regarding the effectiveness of current surface cleaning solutions. With commercial-grade formulas specifically designed to tackle grease, stains, and grime, Mr. Clean PRO offers businesses a reliable option to enhance their cleaning operations.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at Procter & Gamble PRO, emphasized the importance of providing solutions that meet the needs of professionals. He stated, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' This sentiment reflects a growing demand for effective cleaning solutions that allow businesses to maintain high standards of cleanliness and efficiency.The new Mr. Clean PRO line includes several innovative products tailored for commercial use, such as the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are specifically engineered to deliver the same dependability and results that the Mr. Clean brand is known for in household cleaning, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through various distributors and retailers, both in stores and online. With sizes ranging from 1-gallon to 32oz, businesses can easily access the cleaning solutions they need to keep operations running smoothly. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G EXPANDS BRAND ENGAGEMENT THROUGH WNBA PARTNERSHIP

Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) has announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the Women’s National Basketball Association (WNBA) that aims to enhance brand relevance and engage a broader audience. This strategic collaboration is set to include various flagship P&G brands in athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling campaigns, marking a significant step for the company within the realm of women’s sports.The partnership, revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, is designed to deepen engagement with professional women's basketball and its rapidly growing fan base. As P&G increasingly focuses on brand relevance and emotional connections rather than just product placement on shelves, this initiative aligns with current trends in sports marketing and digital media. By tapping into the rising visibility of women's sports, P&G aims to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably in a competitive marketplace.Looking ahead, the effectiveness of these WNBA campaigns will be closely monitored by investors, particularly in terms of their impact on brand equity and customer loyalty. Procter & Gamble's marketing spend in sports and entertainment will likely be influenced by the execution and scale of these initiatives. Investors are keen to see how this partnership will contribute to the company’s broader strategy to connect with underrepresented demographics, especially as the WNBA continues to gain popularity.Despite the optimistic outlook, there are considerations for investors regarding P&G's financial health and its commitment to disciplined balance sheet management. With the company’s share price currently trading at approximately 13% below analyst expectations, concerns about short-term momentum, including a recent 6.6% decline over the past month, may raise questions about the timing of this marketing investment. Investors will be looking for clear disclosures from Procter & Gamble regarding the returns from this partnership in upcoming earnings reports.In summary, the collaboration between Procter & Gamble and the WNBA represents a strategic move to bolster brand storytelling and visibility among a younger audience. As this partnership unfolds, stakeholders will be keen on how effectively it reinforces P&G's established brands and appeals to a fast-growing market segment while managing the associated financial risks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP POSITIONED FOR GROWTH AS AIR FRESHENERS MARKET EXPANDS

The air fresheners market is poised for significant expansion, with projections indicating a rise to $19.75 billion by 2030. This growth is underpinned by urbanization and innovation in fragrance technologies. As of 27th April 2026, the market is expected to grow from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.5%. The increasing awareness of indoor ambiance, heightened household spending, and greater retail accessibility are key contributors to this trend.The anticipated growth trajectory of the air fresheners market is bolstered by factors such as a rising preference for enhancing home comfort and a growing demand for products that improve lifestyle quality. Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC, alongside competitors like Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble, is actively engaging in the development of innovative fragrance solutions. The market's expansion is further fueled by a focus on wellness-driven environments and the enhancement of indoor air quality, as consumers become more health-conscious.Urbanization plays a pivotal role in the increasing demand for air fresheners, as more individuals migrate to urban centers in search of improved living standards. This shift in lifestyle drives the need for indoor fragrance solutions, particularly in densely populated areas. Furthermore, rising air pollution levels, primarily from industrialization and vehicle emissions, have created a heightened demand for air fresheners that can mask unpleasant odors and improve perceived indoor air quality.In response to growing environmental concerns, Reckitt Benckiser Group and other major players in the air fresheners market are innovating with advanced aerosol dispersion technologies. These innovations focus on precise and efficient fragrance delivery while minimizing environmental impacts. For example, Everspray's micro-diffusion aerosol system exemplifies this trend by delivering ultra-fine microdroplets for consistent scent coverage with minimal waste.Regionally, Europe led the air fresheners market in 2025, while the Asia-Pacific region is anticipated to emerge as the fastest-growing market in the coming years. As companies like Reckitt Benckiser Group continue to develop sustainable solutions and adapt to evolving consumer preferences, they are well-positioned to capitalize on the anticipated market growth. Investors are keenly observing these developments as they evaluate future opportunities in this expanding sector.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S HARRY KIRSCH JOINS GALDERMA BOARD

LONDON - Reckitt Benckiser Group plc announced that Harry Kirsch, one of its non-executive directors, has been appointed to the board of Galderma (SIX:GALD) as of 28th April 2026. This strategic move is seen as a significant step for both companies, given Galderma's focus on dermatological products and Reckitt's extensive portfolio in consumer health and hygiene.The appointment was disclosed in accordance with Listing Rule 9.6.14 of the Financial Conduct Authority’s Listing Rules, as detailed in a press release from Reckitt Benckiser. While the company has not provided additional information regarding Kirsch’s specific role at Galderma or the terms of his appointment, his experience is expected to bring valuable insights to the Swiss-based firm.Kirsch continues to serve as a non-executive director at Reckitt Benckiser, a leading consumer health and hygiene products company known for its prominent brands including Dettol, Lysol, Nurofen, and Mucinex. His dual roles may foster synergies between Reckitt Benckiser and Galderma, particularly in areas of product innovation and market expansion.Galderma, which operates in the highly competitive skincare and dermatology sector, has been positioning itself for growth, and the addition of Kirsch could signal a commitment to enhancing its governance and strategic direction. Investors will be keen to see how this appointment influences Galderma's future initiatives and overall performance.As Reckitt Benckiser continues to leverage its expertise in consumer health, this new board membership for Harry Kirsch at Galderma highlights the interconnectedness of the healthcare and consumer sectors, potentially leading to collaborative opportunities that benefit both companies.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S RUSSIA UNIT FOCUSES ON NEW PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT AMID MARKET CHALLENGES

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC is taking significant steps to adapt to the evolving market landscape in Russia by developing new products and registering intellectual property aimed at replacing the majority of its existing hygiene portfolio. This strategic move comes as the company faces a challenging business environment, with the date noted as 2026-04-23 11:53:50.836820, indicating the urgency of its response to market dynamics.The decision to pivot comes after a soft start to the first quarter of 2026, as Reckitt Benckiser reported accumulating near-term headwinds. The company’s performance has been impacted by various factors, including fluctuating consumer demand and increased competition in the hygiene sector. By focusing on innovation and the development of new products tailored for the Russian market, Reckitt aims to regain its competitive edge and address these challenges head-on.Reckitt Benckiser's new product development strategy is expected to emphasize local manufacturing and sourcing, aligning with the company's long-term sustainability goals. The registration of new intellectual property will not only protect its innovations but also enhance the company's market position in a region that has seen significant shifts in consumer preferences. This strategic approach underscores Reckitt's commitment to maintaining its leadership in the hygiene category despite external pressures.In addition to focusing on product development, Reckitt Benckiser is also revisiting its overall business strategy in Russia. The company is actively assessing its operational framework to ensure that it can navigate the complexities of the current market while continuing to deliver value to its stakeholders. Analysts suggest that this recalibration could lead to improved financial performance in the coming quarters, provided the company successfully executes its plans.As Reckitt Benckiser navigates these transitional phases, investors will be closely monitoring how these initiatives impact the company's financial health and market share. The ongoing developments in Russia reflect broader trends within the company, emphasizing the importance of agility and innovation in a rapidly changing business environment.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever Plc is contemplating a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to enhance growth in its beauty, personal care, and wellbeing brands. This exploration comes as the Anglo-Dutch consumer goods giant evaluates its extensive portfolio. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions revealed that Unilever is engaging with advisers to assess various options, including the potential divestment of its food business, which includes well-known brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes.The deliberations are in their preliminary stages, with Unilever considering multiple pathways, including a full spin-off of its food operations or retaining select flagship brands while divesting the remainder. However, it is unlikely that any formal decision will be made before 2027. Industry insiders estimate that a transaction involving Unilever's food brands could be valued at tens of billions of dollars, although the company has yet to finalize its course of action.Unilever's food division, which includes products like Colman's condiments and the divisive Marmite spread, has seen its shares fluctuate marginally, trading 0.7% lower recently and placing the company's market capitalization at approximately £107 billion ($142 billion). Under the leadership of CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is undergoing a transformative phase, having already spun off its ice cream division last year, retaining a minority stake that it intends to gradually sell.Analysts suggest that Fernandez's inclination towards beauty brands is evident, with Barclays analyst Warren Ackerman noting that while the direction is clear, the timing for a food business separation might not be optimal given the current market conditions. Unilever has previously divested other food assets, including its global spreads division and snack brands, leaving it with around €1 billion to €1.5 billion in local food brands still to be disposed of.The challenges facing major food companies like Unilever are significant, as consumers increasingly seek budget-friendly options and gravitate toward private-label products. In contrast, the beauty sector has emerged as a robust growth avenue, appealing to a diverse consumer base. As Unilever continues to refine its focus on brands such as Dove and Liquid IV, the potential separation of its food assets could reshape its future direction and investment landscape.

+ UNILEVER'S AI REVOLUTIONIZES BEAUTY & WELLBEING INNOVATION

In a significant shift for the beauty and wellbeing sector, the company is leveraging artificial intelligence to transform its approach to product development and consumer insights. On 28th April 2026, it was revealed that the firm is utilizing AI to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling the creation of products in mere days rather than months.As consumers increasingly seek transparency about ingredients and efficacy, the need for rapid innovation has never been more critical. The company's €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is using AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, allowing teams to analyze brand sentiment and engagement from over 1,000 external data sources monthly. This real-time intelligence informs the development of products that resonate with evolving consumer preferences.Central to this transformation is the deployment of unique AI insights tools that synthesize findings with extensive research and development databases. By streamlining the formulation process, the company has reduced the number of cycles from five or six rounds to just one or two, while the time taken to develop concepts has been slashed from months to days. The claims generation process has also become 75% quicker, significantly enhancing the overall efficiency of product development.In addition to speeding up formulation cycles, the company is tapping into its extensive R&D datasets to explore biological data, thereby conducting complex research at unprecedented speeds. The introduction of virtual cohorts allows scientists to simulate how various demographics will respond to new products, drastically cutting both time and costs associated with early-stage testing without forgoing real-world trials.AI-driven innovations have already led to successful product launches, such as the Hydra Miracle range, which features a groundbreaking ingredient that enhances skin hydration. Furthermore, the company's Damage Therapy range is experiencing double-digit growth, showcasing how AI is not just a tool for efficiency but a catalyst for creativity and scientific discovery. As the Chief R&D Officer noted, structured data combined with human creativity is redefining the possibilities in research and development, paving the way for a new era of innovation.

+ UNILEVER'S POTENTIAL $33 BILLION FOOD ARM SALE TO MCCORMICK SPARKS INVESTOR INTEREST

Unilever Plc is reportedly in discussions to sell its food division to McCormick & Co., a deal that could reshape the landscape of both companies. The proposed transaction, valued at approximately €29 billion ($33 billion), marks the most significant change for Unilever since its inception nearly a century ago. The announcement was made public on 26th April 2026, with Unilever confirming it has received an offer from the Maryland-based spices and seasonings giant, although the outcome of the negotiations remains uncertain.Should the sale proceed, it would represent the largest acquisition in McCormick's history, given its current market capitalization of $14.5 billion. Unilever, with a valuation of £101 billion ($135 billion), dwarfs McCormick, making the endeavor particularly ambitious. While specifics regarding financing have not been disclosed, industry insiders suggest that the deal could be structured as a Reverse Morris Trust, a financial maneuver designed to be tax-efficient for both companies involved.The divestiture of Unilever's food unit would signify a strategic shift away from competing with major food conglomerates such as Kraft Heinz Co., Nestl SA, and PepsiCo Inc. This move could transform Unilever into a powerhouse focused predominantly on household and personal care products, aligning it more closely with brands like L’Or al SA and Est e Lauder Cos. Analysts have speculated that a separation of all or part of Unilever's food business has been on the table for some time, with the company actively exploring options to streamline its operations.As consumer preferences evolve and the market landscape shifts, Unilever's potential offload of its food arm reflects broader trends in the industry, where companies are increasingly focusing on their core competencies. This potential sale could also allow Unilever to allocate resources more effectively toward its personal care and beauty divisions, which have shown robust growth. The unfolding situation has piqued investor interest, as the outcome may significantly impact both Unilever and McCormick's future strategies.Investors and market watchers will be closely monitoring the developments in this high-stakes negotiation, as a successful transaction could not only redefine Unilever's business model but also elevate McCormick's presence in the global market. With both companies at pivotal points in their trajectories, the implications of this potential deal could resonate throughout the consumer goods sector.

urn:contentItem:6JGY-P943-RVSH-W0TX-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Alliant Energy Corporation *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:25ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Alliant Energy Corporation *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, Alliant Energy Corporation's trailing twelve months dividend is $2.08. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.84%. Its current price is at a 0.4% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 4.8% premium to its 52 week low. If Alliant Energy Corporation was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 2.97%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into Alliant Energy Corporation stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,768 today. This total gain of $768 is generated by a capital gain of $525 and dividends of $244.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

Alliant Energy Corporation's dividend has grown 5.8% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

Company Profile

Alliant Energy Corporation is a regulated utility holding company. It provides electric and natural gas services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Midwest. The company operates through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its primary revenue stream is electric utility operations, which constituted about 93% of third-quarter 2025 revenues. This segment is also its fastest-growing, with revenues up 13% year-over-year, driven by rate increases, higher sales volumes, and expanding data center demand. Gas utility revenues represent approximately 4% of the total, with other utility and non-utility segments comprising the rest. The gas segment also showed growth, increasing 4% amid seasonal fluctuations.

value.Title: SC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGY-C4P3-S0P5-J0F1-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:25ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON & SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [2 News That Matters]

+ SC JOHNSON PARTNERS WITH EARTHECHO INTERNATIONAL TO COMBAT PLASTIC WASTE

SC Johnson & Son is continuing its commitment to environmental sustainability through a partnership with EarthEcho International, aimed at combating plastic waste. The initiative marks the third year of collaboration between the two organizations and was announced on the 31st of March, 2026. In this latest phase, the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand its reach by launching its largest cohort yet, empowering young advocates to tackle the pressing issue of plastic pollution affecting ecosystems worldwide.The Marine Plastics Ambassadors program seeks to provide participants with advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement. Young changemakers will be equipped with essential tools and mentorship, enabling them to devise scalable solutions tailored to their local communities. This approach aims to actively engage participants in the fight against plastic waste and strengthen environmental protections across the nation. Philippe Cousteau, Jr., founder of EarthEcho International, emphasized the importance of empowering youth to drive change, stating, "The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world."Fisk Johnson, Chairman and CEO of SC Johnson, expressed optimism about the involvement of young people in addressing plastic pollution. He remarked, "It's encouraging to see young people stepping forward to help tackle this important issue. As a company, we advocate for smart government regulation as a crucial tool in tackling the plastic waste crisis. We're pleased to partner with EarthEcho to support this next generation of leaders in their advocacy efforts."Participants in the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will gather in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance their policy priorities and engage with legislators. The meeting aims to demonstrate the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions in reducing plastic pollution. With over 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, SC Johnson’s support for EarthEcho’s initiatives underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste, particularly as projections indicate that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040.As environmental concerns mount, the partnership between SC Johnson and EarthEcho International signifies a proactive step towards mobilizing future generations. The program not only provides essential training but also fosters a network of advocates dedicated to creating impactful change. For more information on the Marine Plastics Ambassadors and their initiatives, visit EarthEcho's official website.

+ SC JOHNSON UNVEILS NEW OFF! DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL FOR OUTDOOR ENTHUSIASTS

SC Johnson & Son has launched a new product in its OFF! Deep Woods insect repellent line, introducing the Deep Woods MAX aerosol. The new formulation is designed specifically for outdoor users seeking extended protection against insects. On 2nd April 2026, SC Johnson announced that the Deep Woods MAX aerosol is a picaridin-based formula that effectively repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours.The OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol is marketed as fragrance-free and non-greasy, making it suitable for those engaged in outdoor activities. Additionally, the product is touted as safe to use around common outdoor equipment, including fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces, which is a significant consideration for outdoor enthusiasts and anglers.This product launch coincides with SC Johnson's new sponsorship arrangement, wherein the OFF! brand becomes the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing (MLF) for the year 2026. The partnership will feature brand presence at various MLF events and include a sponsored angler team participating in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events, enhancing SC Johnson's visibility within the fishing community.The collaboration aims to leverage the popularity of fishing as a recreational activity while promoting the benefits of the OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol. SC Johnson's strategic approach to align with MLF is expected to boost brand awareness and potentially drive sales among outdoor enthusiasts who prioritize effective insect protection.As the outdoor activity market continues to grow, SC Johnson's introduction of the OFF! Deep Woods MAX aerosol addresses a specific need for effective insect repellents, reinforcing the company's commitment to providing innovative solutions for consumers. This product launch and sponsorship are poised to enhance SC Johnson's position in the competitive insect repellent market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE PARTNERS WITH HONG KONG DENTAL ASSOCIATION FOR ORAL HEALTH INITIATIVE

Colgate-Palmolive Company is making strides in oral health awareness with the launch of the TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab, in partnership with the Hong Kong Dental Association. This initiative, set to kick off from 1st May 2026, aims to promote proactive gum disease prevention, addressing the significant issue of dental plaque, which is the leading cause of periodontal disease.The TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab will be hosted at Metro City Plaza in Tseung Kwan O from 1st to 7th May 2026. This event is supported by PARKnSHOP and Watsons under the AS Watson Group, marking a significant step in community engagement. The initiative will also introduce Hong Kong's first community-wide oral health assessment, aimed at gathering valuable insights to better understand the local landscape of gum health.During the eight-day event, Colgate will collaborate with various NGOs, engaging visitors with interactive activities and professional consultations. Attendees will have the opportunity to learn about proper oral care techniques and participate in games while receiving assessments from registered dentists and pharmacists. The campaign emphasizes the importance of daily oral hygiene habits in preventing gum disease.In line with its global mission, Colgate's 'Bright Smiles, Bright Futures' program will extend to this initiative, featuring a Smile Power Station where smiles will be collected for charity. For every smile shared, Colgate will donate toothpaste to the outreach programs of the Hong Kong Dental Association, reinforcing its commitment to community health.The launch of the TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab underscores Colgate's dedication to enhancing oral health in Hong Kong. With a focus on community care and prevention, the initiative aims to inform the development of future oral care products while promoting the message of gum disease prevention across local neighborhoods.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE’S 2030 STRATEGY AIMS FOR A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE

Colgate-Palmolive is a global leader in consumer products, specialising in oral care, personal care, and home care. To help achieve its ambitious goals, the company has announced its 2030 Sustainability & Social Impact Strategy, which aims to create measurable results and value. The strategy was unveiled on the 28th of April, 2026, and focuses on reimagining a healthier future for people, pets, and the planet.Colgate-Palmolive’s 2030 Strategy is a five-year roadmap designed to foster long-term resilience and growth while supporting the communities it serves. Building upon its previous 2025 Sustainability & Social Impact Strategy, the company has achieved significant milestones, including the introduction of the first recyclable toothpaste tube in 2019. Currently, approximately 97% of its toothpaste SKUs in North America and 98% globally are now packaged in recyclable tubes, with a broader goal of achieving 100% recyclable packaging by 2030. Additionally, the Colgate Bright Smiles, Bright Futures program has reached nearly two billion children and their families across over 100 countries since its inception in 1991.The 2030 Strategy encompasses three primary areas: enhancing social impact, improving hygiene habits, and preserving the environment. Colgate-Palmolive aims to establish a climate-resilient value chain and protect forests and biodiversity while working towards the elimination of plastic waste from its product lineup. The company also seeks to ensure water security in communities and promote lasting hygiene habits through various initiatives and community programs. Moreover, Colgate-Palmolive is committed to creating a healthier future for pets and their owners through its extensive range of pet care products.Ann Tracy, Chief Sustainability Officer at Colgate-Palmolive, highlighted the company's focus on value-creating sustainability. "Our 2030 Strategy targets the areas where we can make the greatest difference, fostering growth for our business and our communities," she stated. This strategic framework is designed to prepare Colgate-Palmolive for the future and meet the high-quality standards expected by stakeholders, including families, employees, and investors.With a commitment to measurable targets, Colgate-Palmolive intends to take a science-based and value-driven approach to enhance resilience across its operations. The 2030 Strategy is integrated into the broader 2030 strategic plan, with new targets set to be announced later in 2026. As Ann Tracy emphasized on LinkedIn, the company aims to drive value creation that fosters long-term growth and resilience for people, pets, and the planet, encapsulated in their 'win-win-win' principle. This holistic approach is expected to significantly impact both the company and the communities it serves.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE ENHANCES RETAIL PRESENCE THROUGH PRODUCT DATA SYNDICATION

Colgate-Palmolive has initiated a significant strategy to syndicate its product-related content across various retailers' ecommerce platforms. This initiative aims to standardize the shopping experience for consumers as they evaluate their purchasing options. The announcement comes on the heels of the company's desire to enhance its online presence while ensuring that product details remain consistent and accurate across digital shelves.The consumer brand manufacturer, known for its extensive product range that includes toothbrushes, toothpaste, and personal care items, has been looking for a more efficient method to distribute content. Rather than manually sending product information to individual retailers, Colgate-Palmolive opted for a broader approach. As of 27th April 2026, the company has begun utilizing technology from Syndigo to facilitate the widespread distribution of product content, which can include everything from images to detailed descriptions.Colgate-Palmolive's partnership with Syndigo aims to simplify the exchange of product data by leveraging purpose-built artificial intelligence. This technological advancement allows the company to bypass the complexities associated with different retailer systems, thereby enhancing operational efficiency. According to Colgate-Palmolive, the transition to Syndigo's platform has already yielded a notable 13% increase in shopper engagement and conversion rates, indicating a positive impact on its online retail strategy.In addition to Syndigo, Colgate-Palmolive has engaged Flywheel to manage the workload associated with its product data. Flywheel's AI-driven software has proven effective in centralizing the management of product listings across major retailers, including Kroger, which ranks sixth in the Top 2000 Database of North America’s largest online retailers. With the integration of Flywheel, Colgate-Palmolive has successfully deployed over 220 products through Kroger’s API, ensuring swift updates to product imagery and descriptions.The strategic moves by Colgate-Palmolive reflect a growing recognition of the importance of synchronizing product data in today’s retail landscape. By embracing these technologies, the company aims to enhance its ability to engage consumers effectively, both online and offline. As the digital shopping environment continues to evolve, Colgate-Palmolive's initiatives may serve as a blueprint for other consumer brands looking to optimize their ecommerce presence.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ PROCTER & GAMBLE SECURES TOP SPOT IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS ON FORTUNE'S 2026 INNOVATION LIST

Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) has garnered the number one position in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, a title it has maintained for three consecutive years. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, emphasizing the company's strength in Product Innovation among a field of 300 leading innovators in the United States.The Fortune ranking, produced in collaboration with Statista, evaluates companies across three critical dimensions: Product Innovation, Process Innovation, and Innovation Culture. This comprehensive assessment ensures that Procter & Gamble's innovative capabilities are recognized in a rigorous manner, placing the company among America's most elite organizations with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion.Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer at Procter & Gamble, stated, "This recognition is a testament to our incredible organization around the world and their commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate." He emphasized the company's consumer-first approach, focusing on deeply understanding consumer needs and employing advanced science and technology to develop solutions that resonate with customers.Procter & Gamble's sustained recognition in Product Innovation reflects substantial investments across its diverse brand portfolio. The company continues to integrate innovation strategies across various categories, including Fabric Care, Home Care, and Personal Health Care. This approach enables Procter & Gamble to leverage its extensive intellectual property portfolio, translating scientific advancements into products that enhance consumer lives.As an industry leader, Procter & Gamble's commitment to innovation is further demonstrated in its robust performance in the Fortune 500 rankings. The company's diverse range of trusted brands, including Tide®, Gillette®, and Olay®, solidifies its position as a key player in the global market, serving consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information on Procter & Gamble and its initiatives, please visit https://www.pg.com.

+ P&G PRO REVIVES MR. CLEAN: A NEW ERA FOR PROFESSIONAL CLEANING SOLUTIONS

CINCINNATI--Mr. Clean is back from retirement—and with Mr. Clean PRO, he’s ready for hire as businesses’ most dependable new employee with a full cleaning portfolio to get the job done. Today, 10th April 2026, Mr. Clean PRO is introducing the next generation of professional cleaning to help businesses clean more efficiently and effectively, making him the employee businesses can always count on.Cleaning is a critical aspect of operations across various sectors, including restaurants, retail, and offices. Mr. Clean PRO aims to address frequent challenges faced by professionals, particularly regarding the effectiveness of current surface cleaning solutions. With commercial-grade formulas specifically designed to tackle grease, stains, and grime, Mr. Clean PRO offers businesses a reliable option to enhance their cleaning operations.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at Procter & Gamble PRO, emphasized the importance of providing solutions that meet the needs of professionals. He stated, 'With Mr. Clean PRO, we’re helping to address those areas of need because choosing the right surface and floor cleaner can make a big difference.' This sentiment reflects a growing demand for effective cleaning solutions that allow businesses to maintain high standards of cleanliness and efficiency.The new Mr. Clean PRO line includes several innovative products tailored for commercial use, such as the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial Deep Cleaner with Bleach. These products are specifically engineered to deliver the same dependability and results that the Mr. Clean brand is known for in household cleaning, but with enhanced performance for commercial environments.Mr. Clean PRO products are available nationwide through various distributors and retailers, both in stores and online. With sizes ranging from 1-gallon to 32oz, businesses can easily access the cleaning solutions they need to keep operations running smoothly. For more information, visit pgpro.com and follow P&G PRO on social media for updates on future innovations and offerings.

+ P&G EXPANDS BRAND ENGAGEMENT THROUGH WNBA PARTNERSHIP

Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) has announced a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the Women’s National Basketball Association (WNBA) that aims to enhance brand relevance and engage a broader audience. This strategic collaboration is set to include various flagship P&G brands in athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling campaigns, marking a significant step for the company within the realm of women’s sports.The partnership, revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, is designed to deepen engagement with professional women's basketball and its rapidly growing fan base. As P&G increasingly focuses on brand relevance and emotional connections rather than just product placement on shelves, this initiative aligns with current trends in sports marketing and digital media. By tapping into the rising visibility of women's sports, P&G aims to connect with younger and more diverse consumers, positioning its legacy brands favorably in a competitive marketplace.Looking ahead, the effectiveness of these WNBA campaigns will be closely monitored by investors, particularly in terms of their impact on brand equity and customer loyalty. Procter & Gamble's marketing spend in sports and entertainment will likely be influenced by the execution and scale of these initiatives. Investors are keen to see how this partnership will contribute to the company’s broader strategy to connect with underrepresented demographics, especially as the WNBA continues to gain popularity.Despite the optimistic outlook, there are considerations for investors regarding P&G's financial health and its commitment to disciplined balance sheet management. With the company’s share price currently trading at approximately 13% below analyst expectations, concerns about short-term momentum, including a recent 6.6% decline over the past month, may raise questions about the timing of this marketing investment. Investors will be looking for clear disclosures from Procter & Gamble regarding the returns from this partnership in upcoming earnings reports.In summary, the collaboration between Procter & Gamble and the WNBA represents a strategic move to bolster brand storytelling and visibility among a younger audience. As this partnership unfolds, stakeholders will be keen on how effectively it reinforces P&G's established brands and appeals to a fast-growing market segment while managing the associated financial risks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP POSITIONED FOR GROWTH AS AIR FRESHENERS MARKET EXPANDS

The air fresheners market is poised for significant expansion, with projections indicating a rise to $19.75 billion by 2030. This growth is underpinned by urbanization and innovation in fragrance technologies. As of 27th April 2026, the market is expected to grow from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.5%. The increasing awareness of indoor ambiance, heightened household spending, and greater retail accessibility are key contributors to this trend.The anticipated growth trajectory of the air fresheners market is bolstered by factors such as a rising preference for enhancing home comfort and a growing demand for products that improve lifestyle quality. Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC, alongside competitors like Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble, is actively engaging in the development of innovative fragrance solutions. The market's expansion is further fueled by a focus on wellness-driven environments and the enhancement of indoor air quality, as consumers become more health-conscious.Urbanization plays a pivotal role in the increasing demand for air fresheners, as more individuals migrate to urban centers in search of improved living standards. This shift in lifestyle drives the need for indoor fragrance solutions, particularly in densely populated areas. Furthermore, rising air pollution levels, primarily from industrialization and vehicle emissions, have created a heightened demand for air fresheners that can mask unpleasant odors and improve perceived indoor air quality.In response to growing environmental concerns, Reckitt Benckiser Group and other major players in the air fresheners market are innovating with advanced aerosol dispersion technologies. These innovations focus on precise and efficient fragrance delivery while minimizing environmental impacts. For example, Everspray's micro-diffusion aerosol system exemplifies this trend by delivering ultra-fine microdroplets for consistent scent coverage with minimal waste.Regionally, Europe led the air fresheners market in 2025, while the Asia-Pacific region is anticipated to emerge as the fastest-growing market in the coming years. As companies like Reckitt Benckiser Group continue to develop sustainable solutions and adapt to evolving consumer preferences, they are well-positioned to capitalize on the anticipated market growth. Investors are keenly observing these developments as they evaluate future opportunities in this expanding sector.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S HARRY KIRSCH JOINS GALDERMA BOARD

LONDON - Reckitt Benckiser Group plc announced that Harry Kirsch, one of its non-executive directors, has been appointed to the board of Galderma (SIX:GALD) as of 28th April 2026. This strategic move is seen as a significant step for both companies, given Galderma's focus on dermatological products and Reckitt's extensive portfolio in consumer health and hygiene.The appointment was disclosed in accordance with Listing Rule 9.6.14 of the Financial Conduct Authority’s Listing Rules, as detailed in a press release from Reckitt Benckiser. While the company has not provided additional information regarding Kirsch’s specific role at Galderma or the terms of his appointment, his experience is expected to bring valuable insights to the Swiss-based firm.Kirsch continues to serve as a non-executive director at Reckitt Benckiser, a leading consumer health and hygiene products company known for its prominent brands including Dettol, Lysol, Nurofen, and Mucinex. His dual roles may foster synergies between Reckitt Benckiser and Galderma, particularly in areas of product innovation and market expansion.Galderma, which operates in the highly competitive skincare and dermatology sector, has been positioning itself for growth, and the addition of Kirsch could signal a commitment to enhancing its governance and strategic direction. Investors will be keen to see how this appointment influences Galderma's future initiatives and overall performance.As Reckitt Benckiser continues to leverage its expertise in consumer health, this new board membership for Harry Kirsch at Galderma highlights the interconnectedness of the healthcare and consumer sectors, potentially leading to collaborative opportunities that benefit both companies.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER'S RUSSIA UNIT FOCUSES ON NEW PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT AMID MARKET CHALLENGES

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC is taking significant steps to adapt to the evolving market landscape in Russia by developing new products and registering intellectual property aimed at replacing the majority of its existing hygiene portfolio. This strategic move comes as the company faces a challenging business environment, with the date noted as 2026-04-23 11:53:50.836820, indicating the urgency of its response to market dynamics.The decision to pivot comes after a soft start to the first quarter of 2026, as Reckitt Benckiser reported accumulating near-term headwinds. The company’s performance has been impacted by various factors, including fluctuating consumer demand and increased competition in the hygiene sector. By focusing on innovation and the development of new products tailored for the Russian market, Reckitt aims to regain its competitive edge and address these challenges head-on.Reckitt Benckiser's new product development strategy is expected to emphasize local manufacturing and sourcing, aligning with the company's long-term sustainability goals. The registration of new intellectual property will not only protect its innovations but also enhance the company's market position in a region that has seen significant shifts in consumer preferences. This strategic approach underscores Reckitt's commitment to maintaining its leadership in the hygiene category despite external pressures.In addition to focusing on product development, Reckitt Benckiser is also revisiting its overall business strategy in Russia. The company is actively assessing its operational framework to ensure that it can navigate the complexities of the current market while continuing to deliver value to its stakeholders. Analysts suggest that this recalibration could lead to improved financial performance in the coming quarters, provided the company successfully executes its plans.As Reckitt Benckiser navigates these transitional phases, investors will be closely monitoring how these initiatives impact the company's financial health and market share. The ongoing developments in Russia reflect broader trends within the company, emphasizing the importance of agility and innovation in a rapidly changing business environment.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever Plc is contemplating a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to enhance growth in its beauty, personal care, and wellbeing brands. This exploration comes as the Anglo-Dutch consumer goods giant evaluates its extensive portfolio. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions revealed that Unilever is engaging with advisers to assess various options, including the potential divestment of its food business, which includes well-known brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes.The deliberations are in their preliminary stages, with Unilever considering multiple pathways, including a full spin-off of its food operations or retaining select flagship brands while divesting the remainder. However, it is unlikely that any formal decision will be made before 2027. Industry insiders estimate that a transaction involving Unilever's food brands could be valued at tens of billions of dollars, although the company has yet to finalize its course of action.Unilever's food division, which includes products like Colman's condiments and the divisive Marmite spread, has seen its shares fluctuate marginally, trading 0.7% lower recently and placing the company's market capitalization at approximately £107 billion ($142 billion). Under the leadership of CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is undergoing a transformative phase, having already spun off its ice cream division last year, retaining a minority stake that it intends to gradually sell.Analysts suggest that Fernandez's inclination towards beauty brands is evident, with Barclays analyst Warren Ackerman noting that while the direction is clear, the timing for a food business separation might not be optimal given the current market conditions. Unilever has previously divested other food assets, including its global spreads division and snack brands, leaving it with around €1 billion to €1.5 billion in local food brands still to be disposed of.The challenges facing major food companies like Unilever are significant, as consumers increasingly seek budget-friendly options and gravitate toward private-label products. In contrast, the beauty sector has emerged as a robust growth avenue, appealing to a diverse consumer base. As Unilever continues to refine its focus on brands such as Dove and Liquid IV, the potential separation of its food assets could reshape its future direction and investment landscape.

+ UNILEVER'S AI REVOLUTIONIZES BEAUTY & WELLBEING INNOVATION

In a significant shift for the beauty and wellbeing sector, the company is leveraging artificial intelligence to transform its approach to product development and consumer insights. On 28th April 2026, it was revealed that the firm is utilizing AI to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling the creation of products in mere days rather than months.As consumers increasingly seek transparency about ingredients and efficacy, the need for rapid innovation has never been more critical. The company's €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is using AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, allowing teams to analyze brand sentiment and engagement from over 1,000 external data sources monthly. This real-time intelligence informs the development of products that resonate with evolving consumer preferences.Central to this transformation is the deployment of unique AI insights tools that synthesize findings with extensive research and development databases. By streamlining the formulation process, the company has reduced the number of cycles from five or six rounds to just one or two, while the time taken to develop concepts has been slashed from months to days. The claims generation process has also become 75% quicker, significantly enhancing the overall efficiency of product development.In addition to speeding up formulation cycles, the company is tapping into its extensive R&D datasets to explore biological data, thereby conducting complex research at unprecedented speeds. The introduction of virtual cohorts allows scientists to simulate how various demographics will respond to new products, drastically cutting both time and costs associated with early-stage testing without forgoing real-world trials.AI-driven innovations have already led to successful product launches, such as the Hydra Miracle range, which features a groundbreaking ingredient that enhances skin hydration. Furthermore, the company's Damage Therapy range is experiencing double-digit growth, showcasing how AI is not just a tool for efficiency but a catalyst for creativity and scientific discovery. As the Chief R&D Officer noted, structured data combined with human creativity is redefining the possibilities in research and development, paving the way for a new era of innovation.

+ UNILEVER'S POTENTIAL $33 BILLION FOOD ARM SALE TO MCCORMICK SPARKS INVESTOR INTEREST

Unilever Plc is reportedly in discussions to sell its food division to McCormick & Co., a deal that could reshape the landscape of both companies. The proposed transaction, valued at approximately €29 billion ($33 billion), marks the most significant change for Unilever since its inception nearly a century ago. The announcement was made public on 26th April 2026, with Unilever confirming it has received an offer from the Maryland-based spices and seasonings giant, although the outcome of the negotiations remains uncertain.Should the sale proceed, it would represent the largest acquisition in McCormick's history, given its current market capitalization of $14.5 billion. Unilever, with a valuation of £101 billion ($135 billion), dwarfs McCormick, making the endeavor particularly ambitious. While specifics regarding financing have not been disclosed, industry insiders suggest that the deal could be structured as a Reverse Morris Trust, a financial maneuver designed to be tax-efficient for both companies involved.The divestiture of Unilever's food unit would signify a strategic shift away from competing with major food conglomerates such as Kraft Heinz Co., Nestl SA, and PepsiCo Inc. This move could transform Unilever into a powerhouse focused predominantly on household and personal care products, aligning it more closely with brands like L’Or al SA and Est e Lauder Cos. Analysts have speculated that a separation of all or part of Unilever's food business has been on the table for some time, with the company actively exploring options to streamline its operations.As consumer preferences evolve and the market landscape shifts, Unilever's potential offload of its food arm reflects broader trends in the industry, where companies are increasingly focusing on their core competencies. This potential sale could also allow Unilever to allocate resources more effectively toward its personal care and beauty divisions, which have shown robust growth. The unfolding situation has piqued investor interest, as the outcome may significantly impact both Unilever and McCormick's future strategies.Investors and market watchers will be closely monitoring the developments in this high-stakes negotiation, as a successful transaction could not only redefine Unilever's business model but also elevate McCormick's presence in the global market. With both companies at pivotal points in their trajectories, the implications of this potential deal could resonate throughout the consumer goods sector.

AMERICAN RESULTS

Xcel Energy (NASDAQ:XEL), announced EPS of 89.0c for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 6.3% sequentially from 95.0c in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 15.1% from $483.0m to $556.0m

- Total revenue was up 2.9% from $3.9b to $4.0b

- EPS growth 6% from 84.0c to 89.0c

- EBITDA to Revenues was up from 29.4% to 31.8%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 12.4% to 13.8%

- EBIT to Revenues was up from 10.8% to 12.7%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was up from 10.8% to 12.7%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was up from 18.6% to 19.1%

ACTIVITIES:

Xcel Energy Inc., headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota, is a leading U.S.-based utility holding company that operates primarily in the electric and natural gas sectors. As a major provider serving approximately 3.7 million electric customers and 2.1 million natural gas customers across eight states, including Colorado, Minnesota, and Wisconsin, the company's core activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas. Xcel Energy focuses on reliable energy delivery through a diversified portfolio that includes coal, nuclear, and increasingly, renewable sources. The fastest-growing segments of its business are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power, driven by strategic investments in clean energy projects and a commitment to reducing carbon emissions, with goals to achieve 100% carbon-free electricity by 2050. This shift not only supports regulatory demands and customer preferences for sustainable options but also positions the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy market. Xcel Energy maintains a strong emphasis on operational efficiency, safety, and innovation to ensure stable returns for shareholders.

MARKET POSITION:

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL), the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Utility company by market cap, jumped $14.34 (or 21.0%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $82.58. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 41.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -20.6%. Average daily volume of 4.4 million shares in the trailing year was 1.3 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JGP-35J3-RSH5-M0K1-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Xcel Energy reports 1.9% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:25ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Xcel Energy reports 1.9% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit

AMERICAN RESULTS

Xcel Energy (NASDAQ:XEL), announced net profit of $556m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 1.9% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 15% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 15.1% from $483.0m to $556.0m

- Total revenue was up 2.9% from $3.9b to $4.0b

- EPS growth 6% from 84.0c to 89.0c

- EBITDA to Revenues was up from 29.4% to 31.8%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 12.4% to 13.8%

- EBIT to Revenues was up from 10.8% to 12.7%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was up from 10.8% to 12.7%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was up from 18.6% to 19.1%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 6%, Revenue was up 2.9% and Net Profit was up 15.1%.

ACTIVITIES:

Xcel Energy Inc., headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota, is a leading U.S.-based utility holding company that operates primarily in the electric and natural gas sectors. As a major provider serving approximately 3.7 million electric customers and 2.1 million natural gas customers across eight states, including Colorado, Minnesota, and Wisconsin, the company's core activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas. Xcel Energy focuses on reliable energy delivery through a diversified portfolio that includes coal, nuclear, and increasingly, renewable sources. The fastest-growing segments of its business are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power, driven by strategic investments in clean energy projects and a commitment to reducing carbon emissions, with goals to achieve 100% carbon-free electricity by 2050. This shift not only supports regulatory demands and customer preferences for sustainable options but also positions the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy market. Xcel Energy maintains a strong emphasis on operational efficiency, safety, and innovation to ensure stable returns for shareholders.

MARKET POSITION:

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL), the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Utility company by market cap, jumped $14.34 (or 21.0%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $82.58. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 41.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -20.6%. Average daily volume of 4.4 million shares in the trailing year was 1.3 times average daily volume in the previous year.
